generated_oml:
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARDataTransmitted
]

instance RawLiDARDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalTwin
]

instance UserCommandDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ControlPanel
]

instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from physical vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputationEnabler, PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state parameters including speed, position, and orientation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance StructuredObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks from LiDAR point cloud clustering"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance UserCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operator commands for ACC control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData UserCommandDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RemoteControlEnabler
]

instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 package for point cloud clustering and tracking objects"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CloudComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based resource for offloading ACC computations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UI-based interface for operator commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for processing data for visualization services"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state, projected path, and object tracks with reporting capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateVisualization, ProjectedDrivingPathVisualization, DetectedObjectTracksVisualization, ReportGeneration
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloading of ACC system computations to cloud resources"
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetectionResult, SpeedComputationResult, ActuationCommandExecution
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operator interface for remote control of ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableDisableACC, SetDesiredSpeed, EmergencyBrakingCommand
]

instance RealTimeVehicleStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time display of vehicle speed, position, and other state parameters"
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of the vehicle's predicted trajectory based on current driving conditions"
]

instance DetectedObjectTracksVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time display of detected objects and their tracks from sensor data"
]

instance ReportGeneration : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of system performance reports from logged data"
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionResult : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Result of lead vehicle detection computation offloaded to cloud"
]

instance SpeedComputationResult : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed speed based on distance to lead vehicle"
]

instance ActuationCommandExecution : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of actuation commands for vehicle control (e.g., throttle, brake)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableDisableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set target speed for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immediate braking command for safety-critical situations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical entity of the 1/10-scale teleoperated autonomous vehicle with sensor and actuator resources"
]

instance DigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Virtual counterpart of the physical vehicle hosting digital models and services"
]

instance User : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Human operator interacting with the Digital Twin via UI"
]

instance ThreeDVisualization : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D visualization component for real-time vehicle state and object tracks"
]

instance ControlPanel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "UI component for issuing ACC control commands and settings"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC), including speed regulation and steering angle adjustments, are generated by the ACC Controlling FE and transmitted via ROS2 network with Data Translation handling serialization and message alignment. Actuation FE components manage motor and steering servo actuation, though specific validation protocols, authentication mechanisms, feedback systems, conflict resolution strategies, and command execution confirmations are not documented."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Requires 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy for computation offloading between physical and digital components in safety-critical vehicular applications, constrained by hardware limitations despite industry standards typically mandating stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture follows ISO 23247 RA with three entities (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User) deployed on separate hardware. Functional Entities are implemented as ROS2 packages with ROS2 nodes. Physical Twin Entity's design departs from ISO 23247 by hosting general automotive functionality (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) and reflecting inherent system resources, impacting functionality ownership and engineering processes. Coordination relies on ROS2 communication and 100 ms clock synchronization for safety-critical vehicular applications."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between software components occurs within a ROS2 network, while inter-entity communication occurs over a Wi-Fi network using UDP in the transport layer. Network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, reliability mechanisms, and security protocols are not provided in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the ISO 23247 standard, which includes four parts: Overview and general principles, Reference architecture, Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, and Information exchange. The reference architecture (Part 2) defines an entity-based structure comprising Observable Manufacturing Elements (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Functional Entities (FEs) implement specific tasks such as data transformation and user interfacing, with OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) cited as a protocol example for implementation."
]
